paper.title: The Catalog of early-type Runaway stars from LAMOST-DR8

review.summary: This paper presents a catalog of 229 runaway candidate stars identified from 4,432 early-type stars in the LAMOST MRS DR8 sample, using radial velocities from LAMOST and astrometry from Gaia DR3. The paper contains four internal LaTeX tables: a sample summary table with radial velocities and atmospheric parameters (shown in excerpt with full machine-readable form available), two object catalogs of runaway candidates (229 stars via space velocity and 480 stars via transverse velocity), and a parameter table with circular orbital elements for two binary candidate systems. External resources include the LAMOST DR8 dataset, a published LAMOST MRS RV survey GitHub repository, and machine-readable versions of the three main tables.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports the Gaia source ID, equatorial coordinates, radial velocity, effective temperature, projected rotational velocity, and surface gravity for each of the 4,432 sample stars. The full table is available in machine-readable form; only the first eight entries are shown in print.

source_refs.caption: The Radial Velocity and atmospheric parameter of the sample stars \label{tab:RV Catalogs}
source_refs.label: tab:RV Catalogs

columns.name: \sigma_r | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Uncertainty in the radial velocity measurement

internal_tables.evidence: The Radial Velocity and atmospheric parameter of the sample stars \label{tab:RV Catalogs}
internal_tables.comments: Full table available in machine-readable form per tablecomments.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Object catalog of the 229 runaway candidate stars identified from the LAMOST MRS DR8 sample using the 3D peculiar space velocity criterion (V_Sp > 43 km/s). Reports astrometry, peculiar velocities, Galactic height, and Galactic space velocity components.

source_refs.caption: The Peculiar Velocities of the 229 Runaway Candidate Stars Identified from the LAMOST MRS DR8 Sample \label{tab:Runaway Catalogs}
source_refs.label: tab:Runaway Catalogs

columns.name: \mu_\alpha | \colhead(mas yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension direction (including cos delta factor)

columns.name: U | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Galactic space velocity component towards the Galactic center

columns.name: V | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Galactic space velocity component in the direction of Galactic rotation

columns.name: W | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Galactic space velocity component towards the North Galactic Pole

columns.name: Flag
columns.meaning: Flag indicating identification method overlap: S = identified by both space and transverse velocity methods, N = identified only by this method, B = possible binary

internal_tables.evidence: The Peculiar Velocities of the 229 Runaway Candidate Stars Identified from the LAMOST MRS DR8 Sample \label{tab:Runaway Catalogs}
internal_tables.comments: Full table available in machine-readable form. Overlap with Table 3 (transverse-velocity candidates) marked with Flag=S.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Object catalog of the 480 runaway candidate stars identified using the transverse velocity criterion (V_Tp > 42 km/s). Columns are identical to Table 2 but for a larger sample identified via 2D kinematics alone.

source_refs.caption: The 480 Runaway Candidate Stars based on transverse velocity \label{tab:Runaway Catalogs Rt}
source_refs.label: tab:Runaway Catalogs Rt

columns.name: \mu_\alpha | \colhead(mas yr^-1)

columns.name: V | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Galactic space velocity component in the direction of Galactic rotation

columns.meaning: Galactic space velocity component towards the North Galactic Pole

columns.name: Flag
columns.meaning: Flag indicating identification method overlap: S = identified by both space and transverse velocity methods, N = identified only by this method

internal_tables.evidence: The 480 Runaway Candidate Stars based on transverse velocity \label{tab:Runaway Catalogs Rt}
internal_tables.comments: Full table available in machine-readable form. Common stars with Table 2 marked with Flag=S.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports the circular orbital elements of two binary candidate systems among the runaway sample, derived from RV measurements using a global minimization technique.

source_refs.caption: Circular orbital elements of the two binary candidate systems \label{tab:orbit_binary}
source_refs.label: tab:orbit_binary

columns.name: Value | A
columns.meaning: Value of the orbital element for binary candidate A (Gaia Source ID 454293469890174976)

columns.name: B
columns.meaning: Value of the orbital element for binary candidate B (Gaia Source ID 3453566198941548160)

internal_tables.evidence: Circular orbital elements of the two binary candidate systems \label{tab:orbit_binary}
internal_tables.comments: Rows include orbital period, epoch of superior conjunction, semi-amplitude, systemic velocity, mass function, projected semi-major axis, and rms residual.

external_resources.description: LAMOST DR8 dataset comprising 16.6 million spectra and 7.91 million sets of estimated stellar parameters.

source_refs.context: The eighth data release (DR8) dataset \footnote{\url{http://www.lamost.org/dr8/}} comprises 16.6 million spectra and provides 7.91 million sets of estimated stellar parameters.

external_resources.evidence: The MRS spectra are made with blue and red cameras. The eighth data release (DR8) dataset \footnote{\url{http://www.lamost.org/dr8/}} comprises 16.6 million spectra and provides 7.91 million sets of estimated stellar parameters.

external_resources.description: Published LAMOST MRS radial velocity survey released by Zhang et al. (2021), containing absolute RV measurements for more than 0.8 million stars.

source_refs.context: We cross-matched the published LAMOST MRS radial velocity (RV) survey\footnote{\url{https://github.com/hypergravity/paperdata}} released by \citet{2021zhangboRV} to collect the Doppler shifts of the sample stars.

external_resources.evidence: We cross-matched the published LAMOST MRS radial velocity (RV) survey\footnote{\url{https://github.com/hypergravity/paperdata}} released by \citet{2021zhangboRV} to collect the Doppler shifts of the sample stars.
